content.claim: Even absent formal early-selection tracks, Hungarian schools engage in substantial within-school classroom sorting by socioeconomic status, which has significant effects on test-score inequality; sorting would likely relocate rather than be eliminated if the 6-/8-year gimnázium structure were abolished.
content.context: Fresh live research through the Equity and social mobility lens
limitations: Sorting effect is demonstrated but the counterfactual (what sorting would look like without formal tracks) is not directly tested
Causal claim about relocation of sorting is an inference rather than an empirical finding in the source
Magnitude of overlap between formal-track and within-school sorting effects is not quantified
content.population: Population described in the cited finding
content.time_scope: As reported by the cited source
